Node started: Given the initial tree and the final tree, identify the set of nodes that were merged into single nodes. Specifically, for each node in the final tree, identify which set of nodes from the initial tree it represents. Output a mapping of final-tree-nodes to their constituent initial-tree-nodes.

Node started: Using the mapping of final-tree-nodes to initial-tree-nodes, determine the valid sequence of sibling-merge operations. A merge operation takes two adjacent siblings and combines them. For each node in the final tree that represents multiple initial nodes, calculate the sequence of merges required to combine its initial-tree-node children into the final configuration, ensuring the order respects the sibling adjacency in the initial tree.
